title: Rebuild product Pod image on a SINGLE cu130 base (collapse cu124/cu128 split) + multi-arch sage
description:
THE cu130 SHIP (from MPI-187, live-proven 2026-07-04: cu130 collapses the LTX fault-in ~10x, 108-127s -> ~11s). Rebuild mpi-ci/cubric-vision-pod/Dockerfile onto ONE cu130 profile, replacing the current two-profile split (cu124/cu126 broad + cu128 Blackwell). Everything else (comfy-aimdo 0.4.10, comfy-kitchen, ComfyUI 0.27, our node set, wrapper) stays; ONLY the torch/CUDA base moves.

=== WHY ONE IMAGE (coverage fear = FALSE) ===
Live-checked the RunPod deploy list with the cu130 filter applied: RTX 4090 (Ada sm_89) AND RTX 5090 (Blackwell sm_120) BOTH show available/High + PRO 4000/4500/6000. cu130 needs driver r580+, but the cards we actually deploy on all have it; only genuinely-old hosts (mostly Unavailable anyway) drop off. torch 2.10+cu130 wheels carry BOTH sm_89 and sm_120 in one wheel (RunPod's single cuda13.0 image ran the 5090 fine and would run a 4090). So one image serves every card we use -> delete the podImageForCard two-profile branching entirely (also removes the enum-desync / wrong-profile bug class, cf MPI-135/MPI-70).

=== REFERENCE STACK (pin to this — from MPI-187, the ~11s Pod) ===
torch 2.10.0+cu130, torchvision 0.25.0+cu130, torchaudio 2.10.0+cu130 (--index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu130); CUDA 13.0; driver floor r580; py3.12; ComfyUI 0.27 (our current core, NOT RunPod's 0.26); comfy-aimdo 0.4.10 (unchanged, still load-bearing); triton (torch-bundled). Base image: pick a cu130 devel base (RunPod's runpod/pytorch cu130 or an nvidia/cuda:13.0 devel + pip torch). The Dockerfile line ~28 already anticipates 'a future cu130 profile is an ADDITIVE third (BASE_IMAGE, CUDA_PROFILE, tag)' — but per the single-image decision, ADD cu130 then REMOVE the cu124+cu128 branches (torch install block ~L136-145, sage block ~L165-171).

=== SAGEATTENTION — multi-arch build (the one real technical step) ===
Sage is a per-arch SOURCE build (MPI-145): compiles CUDA kernels for exactly the arches in TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST; a missing arch = silent SDPA fallback (slower) on that card. Current: cu124->'8.6;8.9', cu128->'12.0'. For ONE cu130 image serving all cards, set:
    cu130 -> TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST="8.6;8.9;12.0"   (Ampere + Ada + Blackwell in one build)
Implications: (a) ~3x sage compile time + bigger sage binary (superset of arches) — acceptable. (b) It is a git+ source build (thu-ml/SageAttention) — MUST compile clean against CUDA13/torch2.10; sage can lag a new toolkit. DE-RISK: pin sage to a KNOWN-cu130-good commit, NOT HEAD. (c) NON-BLOCKING: RunPod's stock cuda13.0 image shipped NO sage yet still gave the ~11s fault-in + fast sampling — so sage is a BONUS (~2-3x sampler) on top of the cu130 win, independent of it. If sage won't build on cu130 yet, SHIP cu130 anyway (graceful SDPA fallback, line ~179), add sage when it catches up.

=== KILL STALE REASONING IN THE DOCKERFILE ===
The cu124 profile carries a long MPI-156 comment (~L84-111) arguing 'keep cu126 to sidestep the r580 floor; the torch FRAMEWORK is the lever, NOT cu130 the toolkit.' MPI-187 DISPROVED exactly this (torch 2.11+cu126 = 0% gain; cu130 = 10x). Delete that reasoning block + the 'TODO rename cu124->cu126' (~L111, moot — the profile is deleted).

=== BUILD / SHIP ===
Use the build-pod-image skill. cu130 torch stack is large — watch for the CI-runner overflow the cu128 build hit (build-cu128-v040.log); may need LOCAL build (build-pod-image Flow B) like the Builder. After build+push, bump the app's Pod image tag (routes/remotePodLifecycle.js podImageForCard -> now returns ONE cu130 tag) + refresh the RunPod template. Update docs/builder/02-image-and-rebuild.md + the CUDA-matrix note (product Pod=cu130, Builder=cu130, local=cu130 -> all cu130 now).

=== SEQUENCE ===
GATED ON MPI-186: do NOT build this image until MPI-186 settles the image ARCHITECTURE (bake-and-cache vs shrink vs registry — its decision spike). 186 decides HOW to structure the image; THIS card builds that structure ON cu130. Building 189 before 186 concludes = rebuild twice. Full order: MPI-186 (decide architecture on the current image) -> MPI-189 (build the single cu130 image THAT way) -> MPI-188 (allowedCudaVersions guard, alongside/just before 189 so the cu130 image can't driver-roulette-crash) -> MPI-190 (bf16 revert). The shrink + bake-and-cache from 186 LAND IN this rebuild (don't rebuild twice). BRANCH: mpi-ci (Dockerfile/start.sh) + RunPod branch (app-side tag + podImageForCard collapse). All live Pod ops USER-only. Cross-ref: MPI-187 (proof), MPI-188 (prereq), MPI-190 (bf16), MPI-186 (shrink), MPI-70 (the split being collapsed), docs/builder/research/pod-perf-investigation.md (RESOLVED banner).
